summary: Treewide assembly cleanup converting many ASCII byte/word literal runs from hexadecimal .4byte data into readable .asciz string directives with explicit .balign padding. The change touched 66 asm files across text blobs, menus, game mode, fighter, item, map/stage, library, and collision areas, substantially reducing line count while preserving labels and alignment.
what_changed: Replaced large runs of ASCII encoded as .4byte hexadecimal constants with literal .asciz strings.
Inserted .balign 4 after most converted strings to preserve 4-byte alignment expected by adjacent labels, jump tables, or data.
Used .balign 8 in places where the original padding implied 8-byte alignment, such as after "Maril", "%s\n", and some longer table string groups.
Converted debug/assertion strings such as "Item=%d Foods=%d...", "fp->item_gobj != NULL", "ftGetParasolStatus(gobj) != FtParasol_None", and "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)" into readable form.
Converted asset/archive and symbol-name strings such as "GmKumite.dat", "gmKumiteSystemTable10man", "ScGamTraining_scene_models", "SdTrain.dat", "ScGamTour_scene_data", and "SIS_TournamentData".
Converted many stage asset paths and internal object names, for example "/GrBb.dat", "/GrCs.dat", "start_jobj", "end_jobj", "map_gobj", and "translate".

Converted Pokémon/item/debug name tables in asm/melee/db/code_80225374.s, including strings like "Random", "Kabigon", "Kamex", "Fire", "Thunder", "Freezer", "Sonans", "Hassam", "Unknown", "Entei", "Raikou", "Suikun", "Lugia", "Houou", "Metamon", "Pippi", "Togepy", "Mew", "Cerebi", "Lucky", and "Maril".
The PR changed 66 files with 1761 added lines and 5321 deleted lines, indicating a broad readability/data representation cleanup rather than a narrow functional change.
smart_moves: Preserved existing global labels and surrounding data layout while changing only the representation of string bytes.
Paired .asciz with explicit .balign directives, avoiding accidental symbol address shifts from implicit string sizes or missing null/padding bytes.
Used readable literal strings for assertion/debug text, which makes future decompilation easier by exposing source-like expressions and likely original identifiers.
Kept mixed data structures intact: pointer tables, constants, jump tables, and numeric data remain .4byte while only obvious ASCII regions were translated.
Converted strings treewide in one pass, improving searchability for asset names, debug text, object names, and likely source identifiers across many subsystems.
decomp_lessons: When hex .4byte runs decode to ASCII, prefer .asciz plus explicit .balign over opaque word literals; this improves readability without changing bytes if padding is chosen correctly.
Alignment must be inferred from the original data footprint, not guessed uniformly. Most conversions use .balign 4, but some need .balign 8 to preserve following label addresses.
Readable strings often reveal original source identifiers, assert expressions, archive names, scene resource names, table names, and object names that can guide future function and struct naming.

Do not translate nearby non-string data just because it is adjacent; leave constants, pointers, null sentinels, jump tables, and packed values as numeric directives unless evidence shows they are strings.
String cleanup in asm can be a low-risk preparatory step for later C decompilation because it makes source evidence searchable while preserving assembly matching.
naming_conventions: Asset/archive strings often use compact file names and stage prefixes, e.g. "/GrBb.dat", "/GrCs.dat", "GmKumite.dat", "SdTrain.dat", and "TmBox.dat".

Scene/resource identifiers use subsystem-style prefixes and underscore-separated descriptions, e.g. "ScTitle_scene_lights", "ScGamTraining_scene_models", "ScGamTour_scene_data", and "ScItrNormal_scene_data".

Game-mode table/resource names appear in camel case with subsystem prefixes, e.g. "gmKumiteSystemTable10man", "gmKumiteSystemTable100man", "gmKumiteSystemTableEndless", and "gmKumiteSystemTableMercilessly".
Menu/SIS data names use uppercase prefixes such as "SIS_TrainingData" and "SIS_TournamentData".
Joint and animation resource names use suffixes like "_joint", "_animjoint", "_matanim_joint", "_shapeanim_joint", "_camera", "_lights", "_fog", and "_sobjdesc".
Debug/assert strings preserve original C-like expressions and field paths, e.g. "gp->u.carnull.coll_jobj", "gp->u.carnull.rank", "fp->item_gobj != NULL", and "car_num!=Gr_Fzero_Car_Max".
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace sequences like .4byte 0x4974656D ... NULL with a single .asciz containing the decoded bytes, then add .balign to reproduce the same padding before the next label.
Use .balign 4 after null-terminated strings when the old representation was word-oriented and the next symbol must remain word-aligned.
Use .balign 8 where the old hex words included enough trailing nulls to place the next label on an 8-byte boundary.
Check each conversion against the following .global label or table entry so string length and padding do not perturb addresses.
Leave explicit NULL entries and numeric table values in place when they are semantic data rather than string padding.
review_feedback: No issue comments or review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one review, but no review body or examples were included in the context, so no specific reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining .4byte hexadecimal ASCII sequences in asm/melee after PR 328.
Search for .asciz strings without following .balign in converted asm data blocks.
Use decoded assertion strings from PR 328 to infer function names, struct fields, and source file mappings.
Audit .balign 8 conversions such as "Maril" and "%s\n" to ensure following labels still match expected addresses.
Search asset/resource strings like SIS_TrainingData, ScGamTour_scene_data, and gmKumiteSystemTable* for related undecompiled loaders.
